Text:
1. Tsumo: When a tile is drawn
When a winning hand is formed with a tile drawn by the player, the point value is calculated, and each of the other three players pays this point cost.
If the dealer calls a winning hand, the point value is increased by 50%.
If the dealer has to pay another player, they must pay double.

1. Tsumo: When a tile is drawn
When a winning hand is formed with a tile drawn by the player, the point value is calculated. This is divided between the other three players, who each pay an even share.
If the dealer calls a winning hand, the point value is increased by 50%.
If the dealer has to pay another player, they must pay double.

There are three types of matches in Crystalline Conflict─casual, ranked, and custom. The primary difference among these is that ranked matches affect players' risers and tiers, and thus their overall ranks as well.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Casual Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Losing or winning a casual match has no effect on riser or tier.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Ranked Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Loss or victory in these competitive matches affects participants' risers and tiers. Thus, when playing a ranked match, you will be matched with players of similar tier and riser to your own.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Custom Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Custom matches allow participants to customize their group, arena, and other match settings. Members may be recruited via the Party Finder. The results of a custom match will not be reflected on Player PvP profiles, count toward achievements, or affect risers or tiers. Additionally, PvP EXP, Series EXP, Wolf Marks, and similar rewards cannot be earned during custom matches.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Arenas■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Both casual and ranked matches are held in a variety of different arenas. The staging ground currently in play rotates periodically according to Earth time.

There are three types of matches in Crystalline Conflict─casual, ranked, and custom. The primary difference among these is that ranked matches affect players' risers and tiers, and thus their overall ranks as well.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Casual Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Losing or winning a casual match has no effect on riser or tier.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Ranked Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Loss or victory in these competitive matches affects participants' risers and tiers. Thus, when playing a ranked match, you will be matched with players of similar tier and riser to your own. Furthermore, a match can only take place on selected data centers, which alternate each season.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Custom Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Custom matches allow participants to customize their group, arena, and other match settings. Members may be recruited via the Party Finder. The results of a custom match will not be reflected on Player PvP profiles, count toward achievements, or affect risers or tiers. Additionally, PvP EXP, Series EXP, Wolf Marks, and similar rewards cannot be earned during custom matches.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Arenas■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Both casual and ranked matches are held in a variety of different arenas. The staging ground currently in play rotates periodically according to Earth time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Victory Conditions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
To win, one team must move the tactical crystal from the center of the field to the goal point. If neither team accomplishes this during the time limit, victory will be awarded to the team with greatest progress. “Progress,” displayed on the HUD throughout the match, indicates the maximum progress a team has made in bringing the tactical crystal toward their goal at any point during the match─as opposed to its current position relative to either goal─and thus will never decrease even should the crystal be reclaimed by the opposing team.

If neither team reaches the goal by the time limit, and progress for both teams is tied or the team with lower progress score is currently within the ring around the tactical crystal, the match will proceed into overtime. During overtime, the victory conditions differ depending on both teams' progress.

・Should your team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must keep the entire opposing team out of the ring around the tactical crystal for three seconds to win.

・Should the opposing team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat their progress score during overtime to win.

・Should the teams have equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat the opposing team's progress score during overtime to win.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Victory Conditions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
To win, one team must move the tactical crystal from the center of the field to the goal point. If neither team accomplishes this during the time limit, victory will be awarded to the team with greatest progress. “Progress,” displayed on the HUD throughout the match, indicates the maximum progress a team has made in bringing the tactical crystal toward their goal at any point during the match─as opposed to its current position relative to either goal─and thus will never decrease even should the crystal be reclaimed by the opposing team.

If neither team reaches the goal by the time limit, and progress for both teams is tied or the team with lower progress score is currently within the ring around the tactical crystal, the match will proceed into overtime. During overtime, the victory conditions differ depending on both teams' progress.

・Should your team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must keep the entire opposing team out of the ring around the tactical crystal for three seconds to win.

・Should the opposing team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat their progress score during overtime to win.

・Should the teams have equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat the opposing team's progress score during overtime to win.

Should neither team fulfill victory requirements by the end of overtime, the team with the greater progress score will declared the winner.
If the teams have equal progress scores, victory will be awarded to the team that moved the tactical crystal closest to the goal point.
In the event progress scores are equal, and the tactical crystal remains at the center of the field, the match will end in a draw.
Turbulence also leaves behind a twister at the site where it occurred. Stumbling into these twisters will result in damage and knockback. If your Guard is active, it will be depleted by the twister.

Turbulence also leaves behind a twister and gale sphere at the site where it occurred. Stumbling into these twisters will result in damage and knockback. If your Guard is active, it will be depleted by the twister.

Gale spheres, on the other hand, will greatly increase movement speed. Only one player may use any given gale sphere, and they will reappear after a fixed period of time while turbulence is active.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Participation Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Crystalline Conflict matches can be entered via the Duty Finder. To register, participants must use a job of level 30 or above, and may not be part of a preformed party.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Role Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
There are no restrictions on the balance of roles within a team. However, there cannot be two players of the same job on one team.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Time Limit■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A standard match lasts five minutes. In the event that neither team claims victory in the allotted time, the match will go into overtime.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their base, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay─however, this delay increases with each incapacitation. Players will return to their senses with HP and MP fully restored and all action recast timers reset. Furthermore, they will be invulnerable so long as they remain at their base. This effect will be lost upon returning to the battlefield.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Other Restrictions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The use of the following is prohibited while participating in Crystalline Conflict:
　・Chat (aside from Quick Chat)
　　* Not restricted for custom matches.
　・Class or job changes
　・Gear changes
　・Items
　・Chocobo companions
　・Mounts
　・Teleportation
　・Return

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Participation Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Crystalline Conflict matches can be entered via the Duty Finder. To register, participants must use a job of level 30 or above, and may not be part of a preformed party.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Role Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
There are no restrictions on the balance of roles within a team. However, there cannot be two players of the same job on one team.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Time Limit■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A standard match lasts five minutes. In the event that neither team claims victory in the allotted time, the match will go into overtime, which may last up to ten minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their base, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay─however, this delay increases with each incapacitation. Players will return to their senses with HP and MP fully restored and all action recast timers reset. Furthermore, they will be invulnerable so long as they remain at their base. This effect will be lost upon returning to the battlefield.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Other Restrictions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The use of the following is prohibited while participating in Crystalline Conflict:
　・Chat (aside from Quick Chat)
　　* Not restricted for custom matches.
　・Class or job changes
　・Gear changes
　・Items
　・Chocobo companions
　・Mounts
　・Teleportation
　・Return

While much of the equipment in FINAL FANTASY XIV provides advantages in battle, certain pieces are worn purely for the sake of fashion. Mix and match your favorite fashion items to create a style all your own!

A special sidequest will unlock customization options allowing you to use these favored pieces to change the appearance of other equipment. Combined with the flexibility of gear sets, you control how you wish to look as you forge ahead on your adventures.

The Glamour Guide can be accessed via the interface for each of these customization features, offering a detailed explanation of the various options available to create a style all your own.

While much of the equipment in FINAL FANTASY XIV provides advantages in battle, certain pieces are worn purely for the sake of fashion. Mix and match your favorite fashion items to create a style all your own!

A special sidequest will unlock customization options allowing you to use these favored pieces to change the appearance of other equipment. Combined with the flexibility of gear sets, you control how you wish to look as you forge ahead on your adventures.

The ensemble customization guide can be accessed via the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>? button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> found on each of these customization features, offering a detailed explanation of the various options available to create a style all your own.

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>1. The base item's job level requirement is lower than that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Level 100 gear cannot be glamoured onto gear equippable at levels 1 through 99.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>2. The base item's class or role requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Tank-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto healer-specific gear.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>3. The base item's gear category does not match that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Gear that affects the body and head cannot be glamoured onto gear that only affects the body.

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>1. The base item's job level requirement is lower than that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Level 100 gear cannot be glamoured onto gear equippable at levels 1 through 99.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>2. The base item's class or role requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Tank-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto healer-specific gear.
To determine whether a given item can be stored in the glamour dresser, navigate to the item help window. If the glamour dresser icon in the upper right corner is lit up, the item can be stored.

To determine whether a given item can be stored in the glamour dresser, navigate to the item help window. If the glamour dresser icon in the upper right corner is lit up, the item can be stored.

※A “+” mark indicates the item can be used as part of an outfit glamour.
